title: Daily Incident Audit Summary
content:
============================================================
ARGUS SECURITY OPERATIONS CENTER EXECUTIVE REPORT
============================================================

ORGANIZATION: AIIMS CII
DEPARTMENT: Cybersecurity Forensics
INSTITUTION: AIIMS Hospital
GENERATED AT: 22/7/2026, 8:34:55 pm
TIMEZONE: IST (UTC+05:30)

============================================================
1. EXECUTIVE SECURITY SUMMARY
============================================================
The ARGUS Cyber Defense Platform is currently monitoring 14 CNI endpoints.
Currently, there are 1 active security incidents requiring review.

============================================================
2. INCIDENT TIMELINE & DETECTED THREATS
============================================================
[Incident #1] Lateral movement in Delhi CNI subnet
- Severity: HIGH
- Status: INVESTIGATING
- Created: 22/7/2026, 6:51:13 pm
- Description: Threat actor utilizing compromised admin tokens to pivot from workstation ADM-09 to CNI Active Directory.
- Operator Assigned: analyst

[Incident #2] SQL Injection Campaign on production DB
- Severity: CRITICAL
- Status: CLOSED
- Created: 22/7/2026, 6:51:13 pm
- Description: Slow sqlmap spray detected targeting Database Server CNI asset, originating from external malicious proxy.
- Operator Assigned: analyst

============================================================
3. MITRE ATT&CK MAPPINGS
============================================================
- Technique T1498: Network Denial of Service (Tactic: Impact)
- Technique T1498: Network Denial of Service (Tactic: Impact)
- Technique T1498: Network Denial of Service (Tactic: Impact)
- Technique T1059.001: Command and Scripting Interpreter (Tactic: Execution)
- Technique T1078: Valid Accounts (Tactic: Initial Access)
- Technique T1550: Use Alternate Authentication Material (Tactic: Lateral Movement)
- Technique T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application (Tactic: Initial Access)
- Technique T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application (Tactic: Initial Access)
- Technique T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application (Tactic: Initial Access)
- Technique T1190: Exploit Public-Facing Application (Tactic: Initial Access)

============================================================
4. CONTAINMENT RECOMMENDATIONS
============================================================
- Isolate affected network host endpoints immediately.
- Block threat origin IP addresses on perimeter routers.
- Reset credentials for compromised operators and user domains.
- Run full telemetry scans on adjacent subnet switches.
